_comment: OIDC -> key-broker reference config (PLAN-133 E7). EDIT ME. This is a template, not a wired framework hook. The broker verifies a workload OIDC token, then mints a short-lived provider credential scoped to that workload. NO long-lived provider key ever leaves the broker.
_alg_allowlist_comment: ADR-122 §A.1/§A.3 — asymmetric only; alg=none and alg=HS* are rejected at PARSER PRECEDENCE in oidc_key_broker.enforce_alg_allowlist(). This list is informational; the code is the source of truth.
_nonce_cache_comment: ADR-122 §A.4 — per-jti single-use nonce cache. TTL <= 300s (5 min, RFC 9449 §11.1). Eviction LRU+TTL only. Key is the (jti, iat) tuple.
_subject_allowlist_comment: Optional. If present, ONLY these OIDC `sub` values may mint. For GitHub Actions OIDC, sub looks like 'repo:OWNER/REPO:ref:refs/heads/main' or 'repo:OWNER/REPO:environment:production'. Leave null to allow any sub from the trusted issuer/audience.
_jwks_comment: The adopter wires a real asymmetric signature verifier (oidc_key_broker.verify_signature seam). Fetch + cache the issuer JWKS at this URL; verify the token signature against the kid. The stdlib has no asymmetric crypto, so this is intentionally NOT implemented here — the shipped default RejectAllVerifier DENIES everything until you wire it.
_minted_credential_comment: What the broker returns on success. Mint SHORT-LIVED and SCOPED. Never return your standing provider API key.
